message.content: {"items":[{"title":"Family and Fertility Survey, Germany, 1992","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10564","justification":"Contains retrospective histories on fertility, reproductive health, partnership and marriage for German respondents; includes events that occurred during the 1980s, covering reproductive behaviour and family life in Germany."},{"title":"Population Policy Acceptance Study (PPAS), Germany, 1992/1993","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.4004","justification":"Examines attitudes toward population and family policies, fertility intentions, and family life in Germany, with retrospective questions referring to childbearing and family formation in the 1980s."},{"title":"Microcensus 1987 – Scientific Use File","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11468","justification":"German microcensus data from the late 1980s including household composition, marital status, number of children, and indicators relevant for family life and some health-related characteristics."},{"title":"German Socio-Economic Panel (SOEP) – Cumulative Household Panel Data 1984–2011","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11604","justification":"Long-running panel for Germany starting in 1984; contains fertility histories, partnership and marital histories, and some health and disability indicators, allowing analysis of reproductive behaviour and family life in the 1980s."},{"title":"German Socio-Economic Panel (SOEP), 1984–1989 (Annual Waves)","url_or_doi":"https://www.gesis.org/en/institute/data-service/soep","justification":"Wave-level SOEP datasets for the 1980s include variables on partnership status, marriage, number and timing of children, and health limitations, suitable for studying family life and some disease/health-status aspects in Germany during the 1980s."},{"title":"European Fertility and Family Survey (FFS) – Germany, 1992","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10926","justification":"Provides detailed retrospective fertility, contraception, and union histories for German respondents, covering reproductive behaviour and family life over previous decades, including the 1980s."},{"title":"Family and Fertility Survey, East Germany, 1992","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.10129","justification":"Focuses on fertility, contraception, partnership, and marriage for East German respondents, with retrospective questions reaching back into the 1980s, enabling analysis of reproductive health behaviour and family life in that decade."},{"title":"Allbus 1988 – General Population Survey of the Social Sciences","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13354","justification":"Includes socio-demographic variables on marital status, number of children, and some health-related items and attitudes; fielded in 1988 in Germany, allowing cross-sectional analysis of family life and selected health topics."},{"title":"Allbus 1984 – General Population Survey of the Social Sciences","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13350","justification":"German general population survey from 1984 with indicators of marital status, partnership, children, and self-rated health, permitting study of family life and general health conditions in mid‑1980s Germany."},{"title":"Microcensus 1985 – Scientific Use File","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11466","justification":"Mid-1980s German microcensus with rich household and family variables (marital status, number of children, household structure) plus some basic health and disability indicators, useful for examining family life and health status during the 1980s."}]}
